title: Interfaces, Dynamic Types, Errors, and API Boundaries · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A notification service must accept narrow capabilities, preserve error causes, and distinguish nil interfaces from typed nil values. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a capability-oriented notification boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define small consumer-owned interfaces and use implicit satisfaction without premature abstraction.
Trace interface dynamic type and value, nil interfaces, typed nil pointers, comparison, and method dispatch.
Use checked type assertions and type switches only where dynamic behavior is part of the boundary contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
